summary: Iteration 77 - Payments Runtime Config, Health Summary, Webhook Guards Testing. All features working correctly including: runtime-config endpoint returning payments_enabled/disabled_reason/publishable_key; health-summary including payments_runtime flags; create-intent and reconcile returning 503 when payments disabled; webhook signature validation guards; replay protection model.

minor.issue: Initial request timed out (30s), subsequent requests succeeded. Likely cold-start or network issue.

critical_code_review_comments: _payments_runtime_snapshot() correctly determines disabled_reason based on key validation hierarchy
_ensure_payments_runtime_enabled() guard returns 503 with structured detail including runtime snapshot
Webhook signature validation properly rejects missing (400) and invalid (400) signatures
ProcessedWebhookEvent model exists with proper fields for replay protection (provider, event_id, event_type)
health-summary endpoint includes payments_runtime section with all stripe key status flags
Frontend admin-payments-runtime-banner correctly displays when payments_enabled=false

success_rate.backend: 95% (21/22 tests passed - 1 timeout not code issue)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Payments module is correctly disabled due to invalid Stripe keys (sk_test_emergent is placeholder). All guards and observability features working. To enable payments, valid Stripe keys with proper format (sk_test_xxx with min 30 chars, pk_test_xxx, whsec_xxx with min 20 chars) are required.

GET /api/admin/system/health-summary.payments_runtime_section: PASS - includes all stripe key status flags

Webhook replay protection.ProcessedWebhookEvent_model: PASS - exists with provider, event_id, event_type fields

Frontend admin banner.banner_key_status_details: PASS - shows all key validation flags

environment_note: Stripe keys in .env are placeholders (sk_test_emergent, pk_test_emergent, whsec_local_test). These do not pass the key format validation (_looks_like_stripe_key) which requires proper prefixes and minimum length. This is expected behavior - payments module correctly enters disabled/feature-off mode.
